job_description:
Senior B2B Content Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function and seeks a senior writer to own the company’s written voice end-to-end. As a technology services firm with 15+ years of experience in AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernization, and operational transformation, we serve mid-market and enterprise clients in the US and UK.

What This Role Exists to Do

This is a senior writing role focused on operationalizing and enforcing the company’s voice across all written output. Responsibilities include: - Long-form content creation: Pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, and use-case libraries targeting senior decision-makers. - Leadership voice writing: Producing content in the voice of executives for professional publishing. - Brand voice governance: Auditing published content to ensure alignment with brand standards. - Content briefs: Writing clear briefs for junior team members to execute efficiently. - Quality gatekeeping: Ensuring all content meets standards before publication. - AI tool integration: Using AI as a productivity layer, not a substitute for original thinking.

What You Will Not Own

  • SEO keyword selection (managed by the SEO Specialist)
  • Social media scheduling (managed by another team member)
  • Visual design (managed by the Visual Designer)
  • Distribution strategy (managed by the Marketing & Growth Lead)
  • ICP definition (you write for the defined ICP)

Requirements

Experience: - Minimum 3–5 years of B2B content writing (strong candidates have 5–6 years) - Proven Google rankings for written content - Experience writing in voices other than your own (executives, founders, senior professionals) - Working-level SEO understanding (search intent, content alignment) - AI tool proficiency (knowing their value and limitations) - Demonstrable content outcomes (traffic, leads, engagement)

Background: - Technology, SaaS, or professional services experience preferred

Writing Ability: - Clear point of view, not neutral summaries - Explains complex technical concepts in plain language - Concise writing with no filler - Authentic voice adaptation

Mindset: - Treats content as a commercial tool - Open to feedback and editing - Curious about AI, technology, and business operations - Fast-paced execution

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios of generic "Top 10 Tips" or trend summaries
  • Writers who cannot quantify content performance
  • Candidates without technical/senior business audience experience
  • Writers who only produce self-directed content
  • Candidates who rely on AI as the primary author

Application Process

To apply, submit your CV and two writing samples: 1. One long-form B2B piece 2. One shorter piece in a voice other than your own

If the second sample is unavailable, explain in your cover note.

Contact: careers@2basetechnologies.com

Benefits

  • Growth-driven environment with leadership, innovation, and upskilling opportunities
summary: Senior B2B Content Specialist shapes 2Base's written voice across technical and business audiences, enforcing brand standards and creating SEO-optimized content for leadership and decision-makers.

job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it.

About Us We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process.

The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

What You Will Own - The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline - Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences - Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking - On-page SEO across all published pages - Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data - Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification - The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages - SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing - Link building target approval (outreach team identifies candidates; you approve/reject based on quality standards) - SEO performance reporting (organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, Search Console health)

What You Will Not Own - Writing content (you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages) - Link building outreach (a dedicated team member executes this; you set the standard) - Paid search (that is a separate role) - Social media distribution (separate channel, separate owner) - Content calendar scheduling (the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities)

Requirements - Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role (strong candidates typically have 4 to 6 years) - Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content - Experience managing technical SEO independently - Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture - Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool - Experience working across US and/or UK markets - Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO (AI-powered search surfaces) - Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without clarification

Mindset - Thinks in systems, not tasks (goal: a compounding organic engine, not a checklist) - Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments - Analytically honest (diagnose issues and adjust direction independently) - Collaborative but not passive (works closely with content and development teams while driving the agenda)

What We Are Not Looking For - SEO practitioners whose experience is limited to informational content - Candidates who treat Search Console as a monthly reporting task - Candidates who cannot explain high-intent vs. informational keyword targeting - Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on tools - Candidates with no exposure to US/UK search behaviour

An Honest Note on This Role This role joins as a Phase 1 hire alongside the Marketing & Growth Lead and Content & Thought Leadership Specialist. You will not be stepping into an existing SEO function. The keyword architecture, programmatic framework, and technical SEO baseline are all yours to build from the ground up.

To Apply Send your CV along with one example of a commercial or high-intent page you built or significantly improved. Include what the page targeted, what you did, and the impact on rankings or traffic.

Contact Email: careers@2basetechnologies.com
summary: Strategic SEO specialist to build organic search systems for US/UK markets, driving high-intent traffic and scalable pipeline growth through technical and programmatic SEO.

job_description:
About the Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of hands-on experience in software testing to join our QA team. In this role, you will ensure the quality and reliability of web and mobile applications by participating in test planning, test case creation, test execution, and defect tracking. You will collaborate with developers, project teams, and senior QA engineers to understand requirements, identify risks, and validate application functionality across diverse scenarios. This role demands a strong testing mindset, attention to detail, and the ability to identify edge cases and potential product issues beyond basic functional validation.

Key Responsibilities

  • Review BRD, SRS, user stories, and functional specifications to understand system requirements.
  • Translate requirements into structured test scenarios and documented test cases for comprehensive coverage.
  • Perform functional, regression, and exploratory testing for web and mobile applications across browsers, devices, and environments.
  • Identify edge cases, alternate flows, and boundary conditions to validate system behavior.
  • Log, document, and track defects with clear reproduction steps, evidence, and severity classification using defect management tools.
  • Validate bug fixes and perform regression testing to maintain stability during releases.
  • Participate in release testing cycles and sprint-level validation to confirm feature readiness.
  • Maintain test documentation, execution reports, and QA artifacts for traceability and quality tracking.
  • Support test summaries, progress updates, and QA reports during development and release cycles.
  • Collaborate with business analysts, developers, project managers, and senior QA engineers to clarify requirements and resolve issues.
  • Participate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews).
  • Perform basic API validation and data verification for correct system behavior at integration levels.
  • Take ownership of assigned modules/features to ensure testing completeness and release readiness.
  • Contribute to improving test coverage, testing approaches, and QA processes.
  • Test AI-powered/data-driven features by validating output accuracy, response consistency, and system behavior.
  • Continuously learn automation fundamentals, structured testing techniques, and emerging QA tools (e.g., AI testing practices).

Required Skills & Mindset

  • Strong attention to detail and structured thinking
  • Willingness to understand business logic before testing
  • Ability to identify edge cases and risks beyond happy paths
  • Analytical mindset and curiosity to question unexpected system behavior
  • Clear written/verbal communication for collaboration
  • Strong documentation skills for defect reporting and test case writing
  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment
  • Ownership mindset and positive attitude
  • Solid understanding of functional, regression, integration, and exploratory testing
  • Experience with test case design techniques
  • Familiarity with bug tracking and test management tools
  • Basic exposure to API testing (Postman, REST Assured)
  • Understanding of Agile/Scrum methodologies and QA’s role in sprint cycles
  • Familiarity with AI-assisted testing

Good to Have

  • Basic awareness of performance and security testing
  • Interest in learning test automation frameworks (Selenium, Playwright, Cypress)
  • Basic scripting knowledge (Python, JavaScript, Java)
  • Exposure to AI-enabled applications (chatbots, recommendation engines, generative AI)
  • Awareness of modern QA practices (continuous testing in CI/CD pipelines)
  • Ability to use AI-assisted tools for test case generation, data preparation, and productivity improvements
  • Curiosity about test data management (synthetic/test data generation)
  • Experience with mobile/cross-platform testing (Appium)
  • Risk-based testing and prioritization mindset
  • Commitment to continuous learning in QA tools/methodologies
  • ISTQB certification or equivalent

Education & Experience

  • 1–3 years of experience
  • B.Tech in IT, CS, or MCA

Apply

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, share your updated resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: A 1–3 year experienced Associate QA Engineer role focused on testing web/mobile applications, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and leveraging manual and automation techniques to ensure product quality.

job_description:
Senior Business Analyst

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role. It is not a screen updater role. It is not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modelling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead requirement workshops, client calls, and discovery discussions to understand business needs clearly.
  • Ask deeper questions, challenge assumptions, and validate the true business intent behind every requirement.
  • Convert ambiguous client inputs into structured, logical, and actionable functional understanding.
  • Break large features into modules, workflows, components, and clear dependencies.
  • Build strong system-level understanding involving APIs, integrations, data flow, and backend considerations.
  • Translate business processes into functional logic that development teams can execute.
  • Decompose requirements into use-case flows, system behaviours, edge cases, alternate paths, validation rules, and dependencies.
  • Prepare high-quality documentation, including BRD, FRD, SRS, workflows, state diagrams, data-flow diagrams, API requirement specs, and acceptance criteria.
  • Ensure all documentation is clear, logical, structured, testable, and ready for developers and QA to use.
  • Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product owners to validate feasibility and align understanding.
  • Ensure QA teams can derive test cases effectively from your functional logic and acceptance criteria.
  • Communicate confidently with clients, handle requirement discussions smoothly, and clarify misunderstandings immediately.
  • Push back politely on unclear or changing requirements and ensure final decisions are documented properly.
  • Maintain requirement traceability throughout the lifecycle and ensure zero ambiguity during handover to development.
  • Own the entire requirement lifecycle from discovery to development readiness with high accountability.
  • Drive clarity across teams by ensuring every requirement is complete, validated, and logically consistent.

Required Skills & Experience

  • Minimum 4+ years of core Business Analyst experience with strong analytical and system-level thinking.
  • Ability to break down complex requirements into clear components, workflows, and functional logic.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication with confidence in client interactions and clarifications.
  • Proven experience in preparing SRS, BRD, FRD, workflows, and acceptance criteria with high clarity.
  • Ability to challenge assumptions politely, ask the right questions, and ensure zero ambiguity.
  • Strong collaboration experience with development, QA, architects, and cross-functional teams.
  • Understanding of API behaviours, integrations, data flows, and multi-module system interactions.
  • Ability to run requirement discussions, workshops, and independently lead discovery sessions.
  • Exposure to SaaS, scalable products, automation, or AI-supported systems is an added advantage.
  • Demonstrated ownership of requirements end-to-end, ensuring developer-ready and testable outputs.
  • Not suitable for UI-only thinkers, passive communicators, template-based writers, or those relying on AI for answers.
  • Success is defined by clear, testable requirements, zero rework, strong cross-team alignment, and confident communication.
  • Candidates must align with 2Base’s structured thinking, clarity-driven culture, and transformation of the BA function.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or related field
  • 4 to 8 years of experience as a Business Analyst or Product Analyst
  • Experience in SaaS or scalable product environments
  • Exposure to AI/Automation, integrations, or multi-module systems
  • Ability to run independent client workshops
  • Must have independently handled requirement ownership for at least one full project lifecycle

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior Business Analyst at 2Base drives requirement clarity, system modeling, and cross-team alignment. Owns end-to-end functional logic for scalable product development.

job_description:
Cloud & Infrastructure Engineer (Firewall, SAP, PAM)

We’re seeking a hands-on engineer with proven experience in cloud infrastructure, network security/firewall configuration, a practical understanding of SAP landscapes, and Privileged Access Management (PAM) operations. You will design, secure, and optimize hybrid environments, enforce least-privilege across critical systems, and support SAP availability, performance, and compliance requirements.

Key Responsibilities:

Cloud Infrastructure (AWS/Azure/GCP) - Design, deploy, and maintain cloud landing zones using IaC (Terraform/CloudFormation/Bicep) and CI/CD pipelines. - Implement network segmentation (VPC/VNet), routing, security groups/NSGs, and hybrid connectivity (VPN/ExpressRoute/Direct Connect). - Monitor and optimize performance, cost, and reliability; apply autoscaling, backup/restore, DR strategies, and patching baselines. - Enforce cloud security best practices (identity, key management, encryption at rest/in transit, logging/monitoring).

Firewall & Network Security - Configure, harden, and maintain enterprise firewalls (e.g., Palo Alto, Fortinet, Check Point) including policies, NAT, routing, zones, and objects. - Implement content inspection (App-ID, IPS/IDS), SSL decryption where appropriate, and micro-segmentation. - Conduct rule reviews, cleanup, and change management aligned to least-privilege; respond to and remediate security incidents. - Integrate firewalls with SIEM/SOAR for monitoring and automated response.

SAP (Basis & Infrastructure Perspective) - Support SAP system landscape (DEV/QA/PRD) from infra side: sizing, OS/DB basics, HA/DR, backups, and performance troubleshooting. - Coordinate with SAP Basis/application teams on transport strategy, interface connectivity, and secure network paths to SAP services. - Implement and validate SAP-specific network and identity controls (e.g., secure RFCs, SNC, SAPRouter hardening, certificate management). - Ensure infrastructure changes do not impact SAP SLAs; participate in cutovers and maintenance windows.

Privileged Access Management (PAM) - Deploy and administer PAM solutions (e.g., CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea), vaulting privileged credentials, onboarding target systems, and managing session recording. - Implement least-privilege policies, JIT access, MFA/strong authentication, and periodic access reviews with audit-ready evidence. - Integrate PAM with directories/IDP and critical platforms (firewalls, servers, databases, SAP). - Monitor PAM logs/alerts, remediate misconfigurations, and run continuous improvement cycles.

Governance, Risk & Compliance - Document architectures, runbooks, and standard operating procedures. - Maintain compliance with internal policies and external frameworks (e.g., ISO 27001, NIST CSF, SOC 2); support audits with evidence. - Participate in security assessments, threat modeling, and incident response drills. - Drive continuous improvement: automation, standardization, and measurable risk reduction.

Required Qualifications:

Experience: 3–5 years in cloud infrastructure & network security, including hands-on firewall configuration; exposure to SAP landscapes; operational experience with a PAM tool.

Technical Skills: - Cloud: VPC/VNet, IAM, KMS, security groups/NSGs, load balancers, autoscaling, backup/DR, IaC (Terraform/CloudFormation/Bicep), CI/CD. - Network/Firewall: L3/L4/L7 policy design, NAT, routing, VPN, site-to-site, SSL decryption, IPS/IDS, log forwarding/SIEM. - SAP: Basic Basis/infrastructure understanding (S/4HANA or ECC), SAPRouter/SNC, OS/DB fundamentals, HA/DR, performance troubleshooting. - PAM: Vaulting, policy setup, session management/recording, onboarding systems, access reviews, integrations with AD/IDP. - Scripting/Automation: Python/PowerShell/Bash for ops automation and API integrations. - Methodologies: Change management, incident/problem management (ITIL concepts), RBAC/least-privilege.

Soft Skills: Clear communication, documentation, stakeholder engagement, and on-call readiness.

Preferred/“Nice-to-Have”:

  • Certifications: AWS/Azure/Professional; Palo Alto PCNSA/PCNSE; Fortinet NSE; CyberArk Trustee/Defender; SAP Technology Associate; ISO 27001 Lead Implementer/Auditor.
  • Experience with Kubernetes, container security, WAF, Web gateways, ZTNA/SASE.
  • Familiarity with SIEM (e.g., Splunk, Sentinel), vulnerability management (Qualys/Nessus), and EDR/XDR.
  • Experience with regulatory requirements in [your region], and audit support.

Key Competencies:

  • Security-by-design mindset; strong troubleshooting and root-cause analysis.
  • Structured documentation and runbook creation; change control discipline.
  • Cross-functional collaboration with application, security, and infrastructure teams.
  • Ownership and accountability for uptime, performance, and security outcomes.

Performance Indicators (KPIs):

  • Firewall rule hygiene (e.g., reduction in overly-permissive rules by X%).
  • Mean time to detect/respond (MTTD/MTTR) for infra/security incidents.
  • PAM onboarding coverage (percent of privileged accounts/systems vaulted).
  • SAP infra change success rate and SLA adherence.
  • Cost optimization and reliability metrics in cloud (e.g., rightsizing savings, backup/DR test success).
  • Audit readiness: evidence quality and number of nonconformities.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at mitty.j@difinitydigital.com.

Brief Description

Experience Required: Junior DevOps Engineer with 0.5 - 3 years of experience

We are seeking a motivated and proactive Junior DevOps Engineer to support and enhance our cloud-native infrastructure and DevOps practices. This role involves working with modern cloud platforms, containerized environments, CI/CD pipelines, and monitoring systems to ensure high availability, scalability, and reliability of applications. The ideal candidate should be hands-on, eager to learn, collaborative, and passionate about automation, cloud technologies, and system reliability.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in building, deploying, and maintaining cloud infrastructure and containerized applications.
  • Work with AWS services (Fargate, ECS, DynamoDB, SNS, etc.) for managing distributed systems.
  • Monitor system performance using CloudWatch, Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Splunk, and respond to alerts.
  • Support CI/CD pipelines using tools like Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and ArgoCD.
  • Troubleshoot issues across infrastructure, networking, containers, and applications.
  • Assist in configuring and managing Kubernetes clusters, including deployments, services, and ingress.
  • Work on networking components such as load balancers, API gateways, reverse proxies, and service communication.
  • Participate in incident response, root cause analysis (RCA), and postmortems.
  • Automate repetitive tasks using scripts and tools to improve efficiency.
  • Maintain documentation for infrastructure, deployments, and troubleshooting processes.
  • Collaborate with development and operations teams to improve system reliability and deployment workflows.
  • Participate in on-call or shift rotations to ensure system availability.

Preferred Skills

Cloud Platforms and Services

  • Basic to intermediate knowledge of AWS services such as ECS, Fargate, DynamoDB, SNS.
  • Basic to intermediate knowledge of GCP services such as GKE, GAR, CloudSQL, Cloud Run.
  • Understanding of cloud-native architectures and distributed systems.
  • Familiarity with infrastructure provisioning concepts (Terraform/CloudFormation is a plus).

Monitoring and Logging Tools

  • Knowledge of CloudWatch Logs, Loki, Splunk for log analysis.
  • Familiarity with Prometheus and Grafana for metrics monitoring and dashboarding.
  • Understanding of alerting systems (Alertmanager) and incident response workflows.
  • Ability to analyze logs and metrics to identify system issues.

Networking and Security

  • Strong understanding of load balancers (e.g., ALB, NLB) for distributing traffic and troubleshooting connectivity issues.
  • Knowledge in API Gateways like AWS API Gateway or NGINX for managing API traffic.
  • Knowledge of reverse proxies and ingress controllers (e.g., NGINX Ingress, Traefik) for managing internal/external traffic.
  • Understanding service-to-service communications, including DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, and gRPC protocols.
  • Experience or hands-on knowledge with firewalls, security groups, and IAM roles for secure communications.
  • Troubleshooting skills for VM-related issues in platforms like AWS EC2 or equivalent.

DevOps Toolchains

  • Proficiency with Docker for managing container images and runtime debugging.
  • Strong understanding of Kubernetes concepts of managing deployments, ingress setups, and pod-related issues and related troubleshooting commands and mechanisms.
  • Knowledge of CI/CD pipeline building tools such as Jenkins, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD for building, deploying, and managing automated pipelines.
  • Understanding of Ingress controllers (e.g., NGINX, Traefik) and SSL termination for secure routing.

Troubleshooting and Incident Management

  • Strong problem-solving skills to identify root causes using logs, metrics, and system-level debugging.
  • Ability to document detailed troubleshooting steps and solutions for recurring issues.

Collaboration and Communication

  • Ability working with cross-functional teams (DevOps, development, and operations) to resolve incidents.
  • Skills in effective and proactive communication to escalate issues and provide updates during shift handovers.
  • Proficiency with tools like Slack, JIRA, Confluence, or Google Workspace for collaboration and issue tracking.

  • Familiarity with infrastructure provisioning concepts (Terraform/CloudFormation is a plus).

Monitoring and Logging Tools

  • Knowledge of CloudWatch Logs, Loki, Splunk for log analysis.
  • Familiarity with Prometheus and Grafana for metrics monitoring and dashboarding.
  • Understanding of alerting systems (Alertmanager) and incident response workflows.
  • Ability to analyze logs and metrics to identify system issues.

Networking and Security

  • Strong understanding of load balancers (e.g., ALB, NLB) for distributing traffic and troubleshooting connectivity issues.
  • Knowledge in API Gateways like AWS API Gateway or NGINX for managing API traffic.
  • Knowledge of reverse proxies and ingress controllers (e.g., NGINX Ingress, Traefik) for managing internal/external traffic.
  • Understanding service-to-service communications, including DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, and gRPC protocols.
  • Experience or hands-on knowledge with firewalls, security groups, and IAM roles for secure communications.
  • Troubleshooting skills for VM-related issues in platforms like AWS EC2 or equivalent.

DevOps Toolchains

  • Proficiency with Docker for managing container images and runtime debugging.
  • Strong understanding of Kubernetes concepts of managing deployments, ingress setups, and pod-related issues and related troubleshooting commands and mechanisms.
  • Knowledge of CI/CD pipeline building tools such as Jenkins, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD for building, deploying, and managing automated pipelines.
  • Understanding of Ingress controllers (e.g., NGINX, Traefik) and SSL termination for secure routing.

Troubleshooting and Incident Management

  • Strong problem-solving skills to identify root causes using logs, metrics, and system-level debugging.
  • Ability to document detailed troubleshooting steps and solutions for recurring issues.

Collaboration and Communication

  • Ability working with cross-functional teams (DevOps, development, and operations) to resolve incidents.
  • Skills in effective and proactive communication to escalate issues and provide updates during shift handovers.
  • Proficiency with tools like Slack, JIRA, Confluence, or Google Workspace for collaboration and issue tracking.
summary: Junior DevOps Engineer supporting cloud infrastructure with AWS, Kubernetes, and CI/CD tools. Requires 0.5-3 years experience, offering 4-6 LPA in Trivandrum.
